pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Chevron's risk-reward is roughly balanced at current levels: the fortress balance sheet (equity $195.6B, D/E 0.67×) and 38-year dividend growth streak provide meaningful downside support, but the stock is priced at a P/E of 23.0× near the top of its one-year range precisely when earnings are deteriorating sharply (YoY EPS -26.6%, net margin from 8.5% to 6.6%). The QoQ earnings inflection (+25.5% EPS growth Q3 vs Q2) and management's confident FCF growth outlook provide some offset, but the valuation premium appears to price in recovery that has not yet materialized in audited financials.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Chevron's risk-reward is roughly balanced at current levels: the fortress balance sheet (equity $195.6B, D/E 0.67×) and 38-year dividend growth streak provide meaningful downside support, but the stock is priced at a P/E of 23.0× near the top of its one-year range precisely when earnings are deteriorating sharply (YoY EPS -26.6%, net margin from 8.5% to 6.6%). The QoQ earnings inflection (+25.5% EPS growth Q3 vs Q2) and management's confident FCF growth outlook provide some offset, but the valuation premium appears to price in recovery that has not yet materialized in audited financials.
key_factors: P/E of 23.0× is at the high end of its 2025 range (13.8–23.8) while net margin has contracted from 8.5% to 6.6% and ROE has declined from 10.7% to 6.6%, creating a stretched valuation relative to deteriorating profitability
YoY EPS declined -26.6% (from ~2.47 to 1.82) on -1.9% lower revenue, though QoQ EPS grew +25.5% (1.45→1.82) suggesting a potential sequential inflection that the market may be pricing

Balance sheet expanded significantly (assets +27.2% in Q3 2025 to $326.5B) likely reflecting the Hess acquisition integration, with leverage remaining conservative at D/E 0.67×, but returns on this expanded capital base remain unproven

Management commentary from Nov 2025 investor day projects ~$12.5B FCF expansion by 2026 and $20B+ annually at $60/bbl through 2030, but audited cash flow data is entirely absent from available financials, making this forward guidance unverifiable

…[truncated 10865 chars]…
s. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items,
3
Table of Contents
net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. For example, as a result of the Alaska Airlines 737-9 accident in January 2024, the FAA investigated the 737 quality control system, including Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, Inc. (Spirit), and increased its oversight of our production and quality and safety management systems. In addition, the FAA communicated it will not approve production rate increases beyond 38 per month or additional production lines until Boeing has complied with required quality and safety standards. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the utilization, discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to monitor compliance with applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations. For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 14 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability and quality of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk
4
Table of Contents
associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments has and could continue to adversely affect product quality, production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We have also experienced significant increases in supplier prices which are adversely affecting our business. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.

Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Forward-looking statements are based on expectations and assumptions that we believe to be reasonable when made, but that may not prove to be accurate.
Forward-looking statements are not guarantees and are subject to risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict. BA.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: Boeing (BA) as of 2026-01-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Latest Q2 2025 revenue was $22.75B, up 34.9% YoY and 16.7% QoQ. TTM revenue is $75.33B.
- **Earnings**: Latest Q2 2025 net income was -$612.00M (EPS -$0.92). TTM net income is -$10.68B. All seven reported quarters show negative net income.
- **Margins**: Q2 2025 gross profit was $2.44B (gross margin ~10.7%), operating income was -$176.00M (operating margin -0.8%). TTM gross profit is -$244.00M, indicating cumulative cost overruns.
- **Cash-Flow**: No cash flow data available for any period.
- **Balance Sheet**: As of Q2 2025, total assets were $155.12B, total liabilities $158.42B, resulting in negative total equity of -$3.30B. Total debt was $53.14B. Equity has been negative for at least seven quarters, with a low of -$23.56B in Q3 2024.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Poor. TTM net income is -$10.68B with a TTM net margin of -14.2%. The company has not generated positive operating income in five of the last seven quarters. The TTM gross profit is negative (-$244.00M), indicating fundamental cost issues.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Weak. Total equity is negative (-$3.30B), meaning liabilities exceed assets. Total debt of $53.14B is substantial relative to the negative equity base. The debt-to-asset ratio is 0.34x ($53.14B / $155.12B), but the negative equity signals high financial risk.

**3. Valuation versus Company's Own Supplied History**
- **Latest Valuation (2025-12-31)**: Price-to-Sales (P/S) is 2.181. Net margin is -14.2%, gross margin is -0.3%, operating margin is -12.3%.
- **Historical Context**: Net margin has ranged from -17.8% to -10.9% over the past year. Traditional valuation metrics (P/E, EV/EBIT, P/B, FCF yield) are unavailable due to negative earnings and lack of cash flow data. The P/S multiple of 2.181 is applied to a company with persistent losses and negative equity.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
- **Price Level**: Latest close (2025-12-31) is $217.12. The 52-week range is $128.88 to $242.69.
- **Returns**: Annual return (from 2024-12-27) is +20.1%. Quarterly return (from 2025-10-01) is +0.9%. The stock has recovered significantly from its 52-week low, but the fundamental path shows persistent losses and balance sheet deterioration.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: Revenue growth is strong (YoY +34.9% in Q2 2025). Gross profit turned positive in the last two quarters ($2.44B and $2.42B), suggesting potential operational improvement.
- **Downside**: TTM net loss of -$10.68B and negative equity of -$3.30B indicate ongoing financial distress. High debt ($53.14B) with negative equity increases solvency risk. The stock's valuation (P/S 2.181) does not reflect the negative earnings trajectory.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- **Risk/Reward**: The quantitative profile is high-risk. The company exhibits persistent losses, negative equity, and high debt, while the stock has appreciated 20.1% annually despite these fundamentals. The P/S valuation of 2.181 is not supported by earnings quality or balance sheet resilience. Upside hinges on sustained revenue growth and margin recovery, but downside risk is elevated due to financial distress indicators. The numbers suggest a disconnect between price performance and fundamental health.
BA.qualitative_report:
### Boeing (BA) Qualitative Assessment

**1. Business/Franchise Read (From 10-K, period ending 2024-12-31)**
Boeing is a major global aerospace company organized into three segments:
*   **Commercial Airplanes (BCA):** Develops, produces, and markets jet aircraft (737, 767, 777, 787) and derivatives.
*   **Defense, Space & Security (BDS):** Produces military aircraft, weapons systems, satellites, and space systems.
*   **Global Services (BGS):** Provides comprehensive lifecycle support services for commercial and defense platforms.
The business is highly competitive, with key rivals including Airbus (commercial), Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX (defense/space). Operations are heavily regulated (FAA, military contracts) and reliant on a complex global supply chain for materials and sole-source components.

**2. Recent Developments (Evidence dated 2025-11-12 to 2025-12-31)**
*   **Defense Momentum (2025-12-30):** Received a significant $8.6 billion Pentagon contract to design and deliver 25 F-15IA aircraft for Israel, with an option for 25 more, extending through 2035.
*   **Financial Outlook (2025-12-02):** The CFO projected "pretty substantial" year-over-year growth in positive free cash flow (FCF) for 2026. (Inference: This sparked a >10% single-session stock surge on 2025-12-04).
*   **Production & Certification Delays (2025-12-25):** The 777X wide-body aircraft is now expected in early 2027 (delayed), and the Air Force One delivery has been pushed to mid-2028.
*   **Program Setback (2025-12-14):** The Boeing Starliner spacecraft failed certification, leaving SpaceX as the sole provider of human spaceflight to the ISS for the foreseeable future.
*   **Ongoing Legal & Regulatory Issues (2025-12-08 to 2025-12-18):** Settlement of a 737 MAX-related lawsuit for nearly $30 million (2025-12-08) and a proposed $600,000 environmental fine in California (2025-12-18). New lawsuits related to past 737 MAX crashes were also filed in December 2025.
*   **Labor Relations (From 10-K):** The 2024 IAM strike paused production and impacted financials. A major contract with IAM District 837 expires in July 2025, presenting a near-term operational risk.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    1.  **Execution & Program Risk:** Repeated delays in key development programs (777X, Starliner, Air Force One) jeopardize revenue and credibility. The 737 production rate is capped by the FAA at 38/month pending safety improvements.
    2.  **Legal & Reputational Exposure:** The company faces persistent litigation and regulatory penalties related to past product issues (737 MAX crashes, environmental matters), creating financial and reputational liability.
    3.  **Competition & Market Shift:** Intense competition from Airbus in commercial and SpaceX in defense/space is structurally challenging. SpaceX's success has diminished Boeing's role in human spaceflight.
    4.  **Supply Chain & Labor Stability:** Dependence on a fragile supply chain and upcoming major union contract negotiations (IAM 837 in July 2025) pose operational and cost risks.
*   **Offsets:**
    1.  **Strong Backlog & Defense Demand:** Large commercial backlog (e.g., flydubai MOU for up to 150 MAX aircraft as of 2025-11-22) and significant new defense contracts provide revenue visibility.
    2.  **Turnaround Narrative:** Management's (CEO Kelly Ortberg) "operational reset" and credible guidance for FCF growth to $10B+ by 2027-2028 underpin a potential financial recovery thesis.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The franchise retains strategic importance in aerospace and defense, underpinned by a massive commercial backlog and recent, large-scale defense wins that provide a near-term catalyst. The narrative shift toward FCF growth under new leadership is a positive development. However, this is heavily counterbalanced by **confirmed, ongoing execution failures** (program delays, production caps) and **material legal overhangs**. The competitive erosion by SpaceX and structural risks in the supply chain and labor relations temper any optimistic outlook. The investment thesis is essentially a bet on operational turnaround overcoming persistent legacy risks.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states the 737 production rate is 'capped by the FAA at 38/month pending safety improvements.' The raw 10-K evidence confirms the FAA 'will not approve production rate increases beyond 38 per month or additional production lines until Boeing has complied with required quality and safety standards.' However, the qualitative report's phrasing 'capped at 38/month' could be read as implying current production is at 38/month, whereas the 10-K language is about a ceiling on future increases. The Dec 25 news item actually states production has ramped to 42 units/month, which would contradict the 'capped at 38' claim if taken literally. This inconsistency should be clarified — the 38/month cap was the prior FAA restriction, and the news evidence suggests production has since exceeded it.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the 38/month cap was a prior FAA restriction and that recent news (Dec 25) indicates production has ramped to 42/month, suggesting the cap may have been lifted or relaxed. The qualitative report should not present the 38/month figure as a current binding constraint without noting the more recent evidence.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states the CFO projected 'pretty substantial' year-over-year growth in positive FCF for 2026 and infers a >10% single-session stock surge on 2025-12-04. The raw news evidence from CNBC (2025-12-02) and the Globe and Mail (2025-12-04) confirm the CFO's statement and the >10% surge. However, the quantitative report states 'No cash flow data available for any period' and the raw cash flow data shows no entries for any quarter. The qualitative report's FCF growth narrative is based entirely on forward-looking management guidance with zero historical FCF data to validate. This is not flagged as a risk in either report — the PM should be aware that the FCF turnaround thesis rests entirely on unaudited forward guidance with no historical track record in the supplied data.
corrections.required_action: Add a note that the FCF growth narrative is based solely on forward-looking management guidance with no historical FCF data available in the supplied evidence to validate the trajectory.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report's TTM gross profit is stated as -$244M, but summing the four most recent quarters from the raw income statement data: Q2 2025 ($2.44B) + Q1 2025 ($2.42B) + Q4 2024 (-$1.59B) + Q3 2024 (-$3.51B) = -$240M, which is close but not exactly -$244M. The raw TTM data confirms -$244M. This is a minor rounding/rounding difference and does not materially affect the conclusion. No action needed beyond noting the figures are consistent within rounding tolerance.
corrections.required_action: No action required — figures are consistent within rounding tolerance.

unsupported_claims: The qualitative report's claim that 'the 737 production rate is capped by the FAA at 38/month' is potentially outdated. The raw news evidence from 2025-12-25 states '737 MAX production ramp-up to 42 units/month,' suggesting the cap has been exceeded or lifted. The 10-K (period ending 2024-12-31) confirms the 38/month restriction, but more recent evidence in the same qualitative pack contradicts it as a current binding constraint.

The qualitative report states the IAM District 837 contract 'expires in July 2025' and presents this as a 'near-term operational risk.' The 10-K confirms the expiration date. However, the report does not note that the IAM 751 strike in 2024 already caused significant production pauses and financial impact, which is documented in the 10-K. The omission of this precedent weakens the risk assessment — the PM should know that a similar strike has already occurred and caused material harm.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing is a classic turnaround story with genuine operational momentum—Q2 2025 revenue surged 34.9% YoY, gross profit turned positive for two consecutive quarters, and the CFO's December 2025 FCF guidance sparked a >10% stock surge—but the company remains fundamentally impaired with TTM net losses of -$10.68B, negative equity of -$3.30B, and $53.14B in debt. The stock's 20.1% annual return and P/S of 2.181 already price in much of the recovery narrative, leaving limited risk-adjusted upside at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing is a classic turnaround story with genuine operational momentum—Q2 2025 revenue surged 34.9% YoY, gross profit turned positive for two consecutive quarters, and the CFO's December 2025 FCF guidance sparked a >10% stock surge—but the company remains fundamentally impaired with TTM net losses of -$10.68B, negative equity of -$3.30B, and $53.14B in debt. The stock's 20.1% annual return and P/S of 2.181 already price in much of the recovery narrative, leaving limited risk-adjusted upside at current levels.
key_factors: Q2 2025 revenue of $22.75B (+34.9% YoY) and two consecutive quarters of positive gross profit ($2.44B and $2.42B) signal genuine operational inflection, but TTM net income remains deeply negative at -$10.68B and TTM gross profit is still -$244M, indicating cumulative cost overruns have not yet been fully absorbed.

The CFO's December 2025 projection of 'pretty substantial' FCF growth for 2026 and the $8.6B F-15IA Pentagon contract (Dec 30, 2025) provide credible forward catalysts, yet the FCF thesis rests entirely on unaudited management guidance with zero historical cash flow data in the supplied evidence to validate the trajectory.

Balance sheet remains structurally impaired with negative equity of -$3.30B (improved from -$23.56B in Q3 2024) and $53.14B in debt; the stock's 20.1% annual return and P/S of 2.181 already reflect significant recovery optimism, leaving valuation stretched relative to persistent losses.

Execution risks persist: 777X delayed to early 2027, Starliner failed certification, Air Force One pushed to mid-2028, and ongoing 737 MAX litigation settlements; however, recent news suggests 737 production has ramped to 42/month, potentially exceeding the prior FAA 38/month cap, which is a positive operational signal.

MCD.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: MCD (McDonald's) as of 2026-01-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: TTM revenue (sum of last 4 quarters) is $26.27B. Latest Q3 2025 revenue is $7.08B, with QoQ growth of +3.4% and YoY growth of +3.0%.
- **Earnings**: TTM net income is $8.41B. Latest Q3 2025 EPS diluted is $3.18, with QoQ growth of +1.3% and YoY growth of +1.6%.
- **Margins**: Q3 2025 gross margin is 90.5% (gross profit $6.41B / revenue $7.08B). Operating margin is 47.5% (operating income $3.36B / revenue $7.08B). Net margin is 32.2% (net income $2.28B / revenue $7.08B). The provided valuation ratios confirm net_margin=0.32 and operating_margin=0.461.
- **Cash-Flow**: No cash flow data is available for any period.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total equity is negative at -$2.16B as of Q3 2025, improving from -$3.80B at 2024-12-31. Total assets are $60.61B, total liabilities $62.77B. Total debt was $38.40B at 2024-12-31.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Margins are high and stable (net margin ~32% over the last 8 quarters). However, without cash flow data, cash conversion cannot be assessed.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: The balance sheet is weak, with negative equity and liabilities exceeding assets. Total debt of $38.40B (2024-12-31) represents significant leverage. Equity has improved from -$4.71B (2023-12-31) to -$2.16B (Q3 2025), but remains negative.

**3. Valuation versus Company's Own Supplied History**
- **Valuation Ratios**: The provided pe_diluted is 0 for most dates, but the range over the window is 0.0..28.2. Sparse history shows pe_diluted values of 26.932 (2025-03-11) and 27.208 (2025-04-11). Using the latest price ($305.63) and TTM EPS ($11.72, sum of last four quarters: $3.18+$3.14+$2.60+$2.80), the implied P/E is ~26.1. This is within the historical range but below the early-2025 levels of ~27.
- **Earnings Yield**: The provided earnings_yield is 38.46K, which appears anomalous and is not used.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus Fundamental Path**
- **Price Level**: Latest close is $305.63 (2025-12-31). The 52-week range is high=326.32, low=276.53; the stock is near the high end.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (from 2025-10-01) is +1.7%. Annual return (from 2024-12-27) is +4.1%. The quarterly path shows volatility: +6.8% (Q1 2025), -6.1% (Q2 2025), +2.0% (Q3 2025), +1.7% (Q4 2025).
- **Fundamental Path**: Revenue and earnings have grown steadily but modestly (YoY revenue +3.0%, EPS +1.6% in Q3 2025). The stock's annual return of +4.1% slightly exceeds earnings growth.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: High and stable margins (operating margin 47.5%, net margin 32.2%) and consistent earnings growth support valuation. The current P/E (~26.1) is below the historical high of 28.2, allowing for potential multiple expansion if growth accelerates.
- **Downside**: Negative equity (-$2.16B) and high debt ($38.40B) pose balance-sheet risk. Rising interest rates could increase debt servicing costs. The stock is near its 52-week high, limiting margin of safety.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The company exhibits strong profitability and margin stability but carries significant balance-sheet risk due to negative equity and high leverage. Valuation is moderate relative to its own history, with a P/E of ~26.1. The stock's modest annual return (+4.1%) aligns with low single-digit earnings growth. Without cash flow data, debt sustainability is uncertain. The risk/reward is balanced, with upside from earnings growth and multiple expansion, offset by downside from leverage and potential margin pressure.
MCD.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: McDonald's Corporation (MCD)**
**Decision Date:** 2026-01-01
**Evidence Cutoff:** 2025-12-31

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the point-in-time evidence, McDonald's operates a highly franchised business model (approximately 95% franchised) that generates resilient cash flow primarily through royalties and rent. This asset-light, royalty-driven structure is highlighted repeatedly as a core competitive advantage (2025-12-05, 2025-12-18). The franchise is characterized by strong global brand positioning, with a focus on iterating its value proposition, operational efficiency, and leveraging technology to improve restaurant performance and data collection (2025-11-28, 2025-12-17). New unit growth is framed as a source of compounding rent and royalty income (2025-12-18).

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **Operational & Strategic (2025):** The company reported positive same-store sales growth of 3.6% for 2025 and a focus on value pricing to address changing consumer demographics and potential economic softening (2025-12-18). A specific strategic push for 2026 is sharpening the focus on the beverage category, cited for its attractive margins, to reignite U.S. momentum (2025-12-05).
*   **Competition & Partnerships (2025):** A notable development is the end of the partnership with Krispy Kreme, which was part of that company's broader strategic turnaround (2025-11-17).
*   **Capital Allocation & Market Perception (2025):** McDonald's is consistently characterized as a "dividend powerhouse" and a "blue-chip" stock suitable for long-term investors, with analysts highlighting its consistent dividend history and low beta (0.52) (2025-12-12, 2025-12-17). Commentary suggests earnings growth and dividends, rather than multiple expansion, are the primary expected drivers of returns (2025-11-30).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Legal & Regulatory Exposure:** The company faces active class action lawsuits, including one alleging failure to provide proper accommodations for expressing breast milk (filed early 2024, noted 2025-11-13) and liability lawsuits related to an E. coli outbreak (2025-12-22). There is also a regulatory threat from CSPI, which threatened to sue over marketing unhealthy food to children (2025-11-24).
    *   **Customer & Economic Sensitivity:** The narrative identifies challenges related to changing customer demographics and potential economic weakening, which management is attempting to counter with a value focus (2025-12-18).
    *   **Franchise System Tensions:** Evidence notes that McDonald's and its franchisees "paint different pictures" of the business model's health, indicating potential friction within the system (2025-11-13).
*   **Offsets:**
    *   The business model is viewed as "resilient" and "healthy and growing" with strong positive GDP impact, providing a structural buffer against risks (2025-11-13, 2025-12-05).
    *   Strategic initiatives in value pricing, technology, and menu category focus (beverages) are actively underway to address growth and customer challenges (2025-12-18, 2025-12-05).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.** The supplied narrative points to a proven, asset-light franchise model with durable cash flow characteristics. Recent developments show proactive management strategic actions (value menu, beverage focus, tech investment) aimed at sustaining growth and market share in a challenging consumer environment. While legal and regulatory risks are present and must be monitored, the consistent positioning as a long-term dividend "blue-chip" with a low-beta profile suggests the market views the business fundamentals as fundamentally stable and the risks as manageable within its scale.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'The provided valuation ratios confirm net_margin=0.32 and operating_margin=0.461.' However, the raw evidence shows operating_margin=0.461 in the valuation ratios, while the report's own calculation from the income statement yields 47.5% (3.36B/7.08B). The report does not reconcile this discrepancy — the 0.461 figure likely refers to a different period or calculation basis than Q3 2025 standalone.

corrections.issue: The quant report computes TTM EPS as $11.72 (3.18+3.14+2.60+2.80) and derives P/E ~26.1, but the raw evidence shows pe_diluted=0 for the latest date (2025-12-31) and only two non-zero observations in the sparse history (26.932 and 27.208). The report's own TTM EPS calculation is internally consistent with the raw EPS data, but the report does not flag that the provided pe_diluted=0 is anomalous/missing for most dates, which limits confidence in the historical P/E range claim of '0.0..28.2.'

corrections.required_action: Note that several balance-sheet quarters show total_liabilities values that are inconsistent with total_assets and total_equity (e.g., 2025-06-30: liabilities=2.76B but assets=59.55B and equity=-2.76B). These look like data-quality issues. The negative-equity narrative is directionally correct but the magnitude should be treated with caution.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: McDonald's presents a balanced risk/reward with strong franchise economics and stable margins offset by stretched valuation, persistent negative equity, and legal overhang. The stock's modest earnings growth (~1.6% YoY) and P/E of ~26x leave limited margin of safety, while the franchise model's durability and dividend profile provide downside support.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: McDonald's presents a balanced risk/reward with strong franchise economics and stable margins offset by stretched valuation, persistent negative equity, and legal overhang. The stock's modest earnings growth (~1.6% YoY) and P/E of ~26x leave limited margin of safety, while the franchise model's durability and dividend profile provide downside support.
key_factors: P/E of ~26.1x (derived from TTM EPS $11.72 and price $305.63) sits near the top of its historical range (~26.9-27.2 from valid observations), leaving limited room for multiple expansion without earnings acceleration
Negative shareholders' equity of -$2.16B (Q3 2025) with total debt of $38.4B (2024-12-31) creates persistent balance-sheet risk, though equity has improved from -$4.71B (2023-12-31)
Operating margin of 47.5% and net margin of 32.2% (Q3 2025) demonstrate durable franchise economics, with 95% franchised model generating resilient royalty and rent income

Active legal overhang including E. coli liability lawsuits (2025-12-22) and CSPI threat over marketing to children (2025-11-24), plus franchisee tensions, partially offset by strategic initiatives in value pricing and beverage category focus

es and accessories. Therefore, many components used by the Company, including those that are available from multiple sources, are at times subject to industry-wide shortage and significant commodity pricing fluctuations. Restrictions on international trade can increase the cost or limit the availability of the Company's products and the components and rare earths and other raw materials that go into them.
The Company uses some custom components that are not commonly used by its competitors, and new products introduced by the Company often utilize custom components available from only one source. When a component or product uses new technologies, initial capacity constraints may exist until the suppliers' yields have matured or their manufacturing capacities have increased. The Company has entered into agreements for the supply of many components; however, the Company may not be able to extend or renew agreements for the supply of components on similar terms, or at all, and may not be successful in obtaining sufficient quantities from its suppliers or in a timely manner, or in identifying and obtaining sufficient quantities from an alternative source. In addition, component suppliers may fail, be subject to consolidation within a particular industry, or decide to concentrate on the production of common components instead of components customized to meet the Company's requirements, further limiting the Company's ability to obtain sufficient quantities of components on commercially reasonable terms, or at all.
Research and Development
Because the industries in which the Company competes are characterized by rapid technological advances, the Company's ability to compete successfully depends heavily upon its ability to ensure a continual and timely flow of competitive products, services and technologies to the marketplace. The Company continues to develop new technologies to enhance existing products and services, and to expand the range of its offerings through research and development ("R&D"), licensing of intellectual property and acquisition of third-party businesses and technology.
Intellectual Property
The Company currently holds a broad collection of intellectual property rights relating to certain aspects of its hardware, software and services. This includes patents, designs, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets and other forms of intellectual property rights in the U.S. and various foreign countries. Although the Company believes the ownership of such intellectual property rights is an important factor in differentiating its business and that its success does depend in part on such ownership, the Company relies primarily on the innovative skills, technical competence and marketing abilities of its personnel.
The Company regularly files patent, design, copyright and trademark applications to protect innovations arising from its hardware, software and service research, development, design and marketing, and is currently pursuing thousands of applications around the world. Over time, the Company has accumulated a large portfolio of issued and registered intellectual property rights around the world. No single intellectual property right is solely responsible for protecting the Company's products and services. The Company believes the duration of its intellectual property rights is adequate relative to the expected lives of its products and services.
Apple Inc. | 2025 Form 10-K | 3
In addition to Company-owned intellectual property, many of the Company's products and services include technology or intellectual property that must be licensed from third parties. It may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of the Company's products, processes and services. While the Company has generally been able to obtain such licenses on commercially reasonable terms in the past, there is no guarantee that such licenses could be obtained in the future on reasonable terms or at all.
Business Seasonality and Product Introductions
The Company has historically experienced higher net sales in its first quarter compared to other quarters in its fiscal year due in part to seasonal holiday demand. Additionally, new product and service introductions can significantly impact net sales, cost of sales and operating expenses. The timing of product introductions can also impact the Company's net sales to its indirect distribution channels as these channels are filled with new inventory following a product launch, and channel inventory of an older product often declines as the launch of a newer product approaches. Net sales can also be affected when consumers and distributors anticipate a product introduction.
Human Capital
The Company believes that its people play an important role in its success, and strives to attract, develop and retain the best talent. The Company works to create a culture of collaboration, one where people with a broad range of backgrounds and perspectives can come together to innovate and do the best work of their lives. The Company is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and to providing a workplace free of harassment or discrimination. As of September 27, 2025, the Company had approximately 166,000 full-time equivalent employees.
The Company believes that compensation should be competitive and equitable, and offers discretionary cash and equity awards to enable employees to share in the Company's success. The Company recognizes its people are most likely to thrive when they have the resources to meet their needs and the time and support to succeed in their professional and personal lives. In support of this, the Company offers a wide variety of benefits for employees around the world, including health, wellness and time away.
The Company invests in resources to help its people develop and achieve their career goals. The Company offers programs through Apple University on leadership, management and influence, as well as Apple culture and values. Team members can also take advantage of online classes for business, technical and personal development.
The Company believes that open and honest communication among team members, managers and leaders helps create an open, collaborative work environment where everyone can contribute, grow and succeed. Team members are encouraged to come to their managers with questions, feedback or concerns, and the Company conducts surveys that gauge employee sentiment in areas like career development, manager performance and inclusion.
The Company is committed to the safety and security of its team members everywhere it operates. The Company supports employees with general safety, security and crisis management training, and by putting specific programs in place for those working in potentially high-hazard environments.
Available Information
The Company's Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and amendments to reports filed pursuant to Sections 13(a) and 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended ("Exchange Act"), are filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"). Such reports and other information filed by the Company with the SEC are available free of charge at investor.apple.com/investor-relations/sec-filings/default.aspx when such reports are available on the SEC's website. The Company periodically provides certain information for investors on its corporate website, www.apple.com, and its investor relations website, investor.apple.com. This includes press releases and other information about financial performance, information on corporate governance, and details related to the Company's annual meeting of shareholders. The information contained on the websites referenced in this Form 10-K is not incorporated by reference into this filing. Further, the Company's references to website URLs are intended to be inactive textual references only.
Apple Inc. | 2025 Form 10-K | 4

**Quantitative Report: AAPL (Decision Date: 2026-01-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
- **Revenue**: Latest Q4 (2025-09-27) revenue is 102.47B, with YoY growth of +7.9%. TTM revenue is 416.16B. Quarterly revenue shows seasonality, peaking in Q1 (e.g., 124.30B in Q1 2025).  
- **Earnings**: Latest diluted EPS is 1.84, with YoY growth of +89.7% (from 0.97 in Q4 2024). TTM net income is 112.01B.  
- **Margins**: Supplied gross margin is 0.469, operating margin is 0.32, and net margin is 0.269. Net margin improved from 0.24 in early 2025 to 0.269.  
- **Cash-Flow**: Unavailable; all cash flow entries are "(no data)".  
- **Balance-Sheet**: Total assets 359.24B, total equity 73.73B, total debt 90.68B. Debt decreased from 106.00B (Q4 2023) to 90.68B. Equity increased from 56.95B (Q4 2024) to 73.73B. Debt-to-equity ratio is 1.23.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings Quality**: High YoY EPS growth of +89.7% is partly due to a low base (Q4 2024 EPS 0.97). Net margin improvement to 0.269 from 0.24 indicates strengthening profitability.  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt reduction and rising equity improve resilience, but leverage remains with debt-to-equity at 1.23.

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**  
- Latest PE diluted is 36.442, within the 2025 range of 27.2 to 40.9. Historical PE shows contraction from 40.107 (2025-01-02) to 36.442.  
- P/B ratio is 55.324, within the 2025 range of 39.1 to 66.3. P/S ratio is 9.802.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus Fundamental Path**  
- Latest price is 271.86 (2025-12-31). Annual return is +6.4% (from 255.59 on 2024-12-27), aligning with revenue growth of +7.9% YoY.  
- Quarterly returns are volatile: -12.7% (Q1 2025), -4.8% (Q2 2025), +20.2% (Q3 2025), +6.4% (Q4 2025). Price volatility does not directly mirror fundamental consistency.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- **Upside**: Sustained earnings growth (YoY +89.7%), margin expansion (net margin to 0.269), and debt reduction (from 106.00B to 90.68B) could support higher valuations.  
- **Downside**: High valuation (PE 36.442 vs. range 27.2–40.9) may limit upside if growth slows; absence of cash-flow data prevents assessment of cash generation quality.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
Earnings growth and margin improvements are positive, but valuations are elevated within historical context and leverage persists. Cash-flow data is unavailable, introducing uncertainty. Risk/reward appears balanced, with upside dependent on continued fundamental strength and downside risk from valuation compression.
AAPL.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Apple Inc. (AAPL)**
**Decision Date: 2026-01-01**
**Evidence Window: 2025-10-31 to 2025-12-31**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones (iPhone), personal computers (Mac), tablets (iPad), wearables/accessories (Apple Watch, AirPods, Vision Pro), and a broad range of services (including the App Store, advertising, AppleCare, cloud, and subscriptions like Apple Music/TV). The company operates in five geographic segments and sells through a mix of direct (40% of 2025 sales) and indirect (60%) channels. Its competitive advantage is rooted in integrated hardware-software ecosystem design, brand reputation, and a large installed base. The 10-K notes the company has a minority market share in key hardware categories and faces intense competition from rivals with significant resources. (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2025-10-31)

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Growth Catalysts & Outlook:** Analysts and articles from late December 2025 identify Apple as a potential "2026 winner," citing expectations for accelerating growth driven by a new iPhone cycle, robust services expansion (15% growth noted), and the impact of AI initiatives. One article specifically forecasts a return to double-digit revenue growth in fiscal 2026, supported by a strong holiday quarter performance. (Sources: 2025-12-31, 2025-12-29, 2025-12-06, 2025-12-12)
*   **Market Position & Sentiment:** Apple was noted as underperforming the S&P 500 in 2025 but was frequently cited as a must-own stock for 2026 by retail investors and certain commentators. The stock has been described as lagging but showing signs of a comeback driven by product cycles and operational resilience. (Sources: 2025-12-31, 2025-12-30, 2025-12-09)
*   **Operational Catalysts:** A key driver cited for the stock's rebound in late 2025 was the receipt of temporary tariff relief on iPhones manufactured outside the U.S. (Source: 2025-12-09)

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk - Regulatory & Legal Action:** The company faces significant antitrust headwinds. The U.S. Department of Justice and 21 states have filed a lawsuit accusing Apple of monopolizing the smartphone market. Separately, Apple is challenging India's antitrust penalty law in court, a case that carries a reported risk of a potential $38 billion fine. These actions represent a broad regulatory shift targeting Big Tech. (Sources: 2025-12-12, 2025-11-27, 2025-11-26)
*   **Risk - Valuation:** Commentary notes Apple trades at a premium forward valuation (33x forward P/E cited) relative to its growth, which could limit upside if growth expectations are not met. (Source: 2025-12-31)
*   **Risk - Competitive & Supply Chain:** The 10-K describes highly competitive markets with aggressive pricing pressure. It also highlights supply chain risks, including dependence on single or limited sources for certain custom components and vulnerability to international trade restrictions. (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2025-10-31)
*   **Offset - Diversified Ecosystem & Services:** The durable, high-margin services segment and tightly integrated product ecosystem are consistently highlighted as core strengths supporting customer retention and recurring revenue. (Sources: 10-K, 2025-12-12, 2025-11-22)
*   **Offset - Operational Execution:** Recent financial results and management's holiday quarter outlook are cited as evidence of a reaccelerating business and successful navigation of challenges like tariffs. (Sources: 2025-12-29, 2025-12-06)

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The narrative evidence points to a strengthening operational outlook into 2026, driven by cyclical product launches (new iPhone), secular growth in high-margin services, and proactive cost management (tariff relief). While the regulatory overhang is material and warrants monitoring, the fundamental business exhibits resilience through its integrated ecosystem and brand strength. The predominant near-term sentiment reflected in the supplied text is that the growth lull of 2025 is likely ending, positioning the company favorably for the coming year.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Debt decreased from 106.00B (Q4 2023) to 90.68B.' The raw balance sheet shows 106.00B at 2023-12-30 (Q1 FY2024) and 90.68B at 2025-09-27 (Q4 FY2025). The label 'Q4 2023' is misleading — 2023-12-30 is Q1 of Apple's FY2024 (Apple's fiscal year ends last Saturday of September). This is a minor labeling error but could confuse the reader about the time span of debt reduction.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that 106.00B corresponds to Q1 FY2024 (period ending 2023-12-30), not 'Q4 2023.'

corrections.issue: Qual report cites '33x forward P/E' from a 2025-12-31 article. The raw quantitative evidence shows a trailing PE of 36.442 as of 2025-12-31. The forward P/E figure comes from the qualitative news pack (an article summary) and is not contradicted by the quant pack, but the two figures measure different things. No inconsistency exists, but the PM should be aware the forward P/E (33x) and trailing P/E (36.4x) are distinct metrics sourced from different packs.
corrections.required_action: No action required — this is a clarification for the PM, not a correction.

corrections.issue: The quant report's Section 5 (Upside) highlights 'YoY +89.7%' EPS growth as a driver, but the report itself acknowledges this is 'partly due to a low base (Q4 2024 EPS 0.97).' The raw data confirms Q4 2024 EPS of 0.97 was unusually low relative to adjacent quarters (Q3 2024: 1.40, Q1 2024: 2.18). The report could more explicitly flag that the +89.7% figure overstates the sustainable growth rate and that normalized YoY growth (e.g., comparing to Q3 2024's 1.40) would be closer to +31%.
corrections.required_action: In the upside/downside framing, caveat the +89.7% YoY EPS growth more prominently by noting the depressed Q4 2024 base and providing a normalized growth estimate.

unsupported_claims: : The qual report states Apple 'underperforming the S&P 500 in 2025.' The raw price data shows AAPL returned +6.4% annually (2024-12-27 to 2025-12-31). The S&P 500's 2025 return is not provided in the raw evidence packs, so this relative performance claim cannot be verified from supplied data alone. The claim originates from the qualitative news summaries (2025-12-31 article) and is not contradicted, but it is not independently verifiable from the raw packs.

: The qual report mentions 'receipt of temporary tariff relief on iPhones manufactured outside the U.S.' as a catalyst (sourced 2025-12-09). This is present in the raw qualitative evidence and is supported. No issue.

No inconsistency exists, but the PM should be aware the forward P/E (33x) and trailing P/E (36.4x) are distinct metrics sourced from different packs. → No action required — this is a clarification for the PM, not a correction.
- [C3] The quant report's Section 5 (Upside) highlights 'YoY +89.7%' EPS growth as a driver, but the report itself acknowledges this is 'partly due to a low base (Q4 2024 EPS 0.97).' The raw data confirms Q4 2024 EPS of 0.97 was unusually low relative to adjacent quarters (Q3 2024: 1.40, Q1 2024: 2.18). The report could more explicitly flag that the +89.7% figure overstates the sustainable growth rate and that normalized YoY growth (e.g., comparing to Q3 2024's 1.40) would be closer to +31%. → In the upside/downside framing, caveat the +89.7% YoY EPS growth more prominently by noting the depressed Q4 2024 base and providing a normalized growth estimate.

**Unsupported claims:**
- : The qual report states Apple 'underperforming the S&P 500 in 2025.' The raw price data shows AAPL returned +6.4% annually (2024-12-27 to 2025-12-31). The S&P 500's 2025 return is not provided in the raw evidence packs, so this relative performance claim cannot be verified from supplied data alone. The claim originates from the qualitative news summaries (2025-12-31 article) and is not contradicted, but it is not independently verifiable from the raw packs.
- : The qual report mentions 'receipt of temporary tariff relief on iPhones manufactured outside the U.S.' as a catalyst (sourced 2025-12-09). This is present in the raw qualitative evidence and is supported. No issue.
- verified_points

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Apple's fundamental trajectory is improving—revenue growing +7.9% YoY, net margin expanding to 26.9%, and debt declining—but a trailing P/E of 36.4x near the top of its 27–41x historical range leaves limited room for multiple expansion. The risk/reward is mildly positive but constrained by valuation and regulatory overhang, warranting a modest Hold with slight upward bias.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Apple's fundamental trajectory is improving—revenue growing +7.9% YoY, net margin expanding to 26.9%, and debt declining—but a trailing P/E of 36.4x near the top of its 27–41x historical range leaves limited room for multiple expansion. The risk/reward is mildly positive but constrained by valuation and regulatory overhang, warranting a modest Hold with slight upward bias.
key_factors: Trailing P/E of 36.4x (2025-12-31) sits in the upper half of its 27.2–40.9x 1-year range, limiting upside from multiple expansion even as forward P/E of 33x is more reasonable.
Q4 2025 EPS of $1.84 grew +89.7% YoY but from a depressed Q4 2024 base of $0.97; normalized growth versus Q3 2024's $1.40 is closer to +31%, which is solid but not exceptional for a 36x P/E stock.
Net margin improved to 26.9% from 24% in early 2025, and debt fell from $106B (Q1 FY2024) to $90.7B, strengthening the balance sheet with debt-to-equity at 1.23.
DOJ antitrust lawsuit with 21 states and India's $38B potential fine represent material regulatory tail risks that could pressure the stock if outcomes are adverse.

e narrowly focused entities. Across our business segments, we recognize hundreds of competitors worldwide and as we execute our hybrid cloud and AI strategy, we are regularly exposed to new competitors.
Our principal methods of competition are: technology innovation; performance; price; quality; brand; our breadth of capabilities, products and services; talent; client relationships and trust; the ability to deliver business value to clients; and service and support. In order to maintain leadership, we optimize our portfolio with organic and inorganic innovations and effective resource allocation. These investments not only drive current performance but will extend our innovation leadership into the future.
Our breadth and depth of expertise enables us to take different technologies and bring them together to solve the most pressing business issues of our clients. We differentiate from other providers by bringing together incumbency with enterprises, deep expertise in technology, industries and business processes, a broad go-to-market reach including an ecosystem of partners and alliances, and a strong focus on innovation. All of these attributes position IBM for accelerated growth now and prepare us for the next set of business opportunities, such as quantum computing.
1
Table of Contents
Overall, the company is the leader or among the leaders in each of our business segments. A summary of the competitive environment for each business segment is included below:
Software:
The depth, breadth, and innovation of our software offerings, coupled with our global reach, deep industry expertise and research capabilities help differentiate our offerings from our competitors. Our hybrid cloud and AI platforms allow clients to realize their digital and AI transformations across the applications, data, and environments in which they operate. The principal competitors in this segment include: Alphabet (Google), Amazon, BMC, Broadcom, Informatica, Microsoft, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP and Splunk. We also compete with smaller, niche competitors in specific geographic regions or product segments.
Consulting:
Consulting focuses on integrating skills on strategy, experience, technology and operations by domain and industry. Consulting competes in a dynamic market including consulting, systems integration, application development, application management and business process outsourcing services. Our broad-based competitors include: Accenture, Capgemini, India-based service providers, management consulting firms, the consulting practices of public accounting firms, engineering service providers, and many companies that primarily focus on local markets or niche service areas.
Infrastructure:
IBM is well positioned in the growing hybrid cloud infrastructure market, providing on-premises and cloud-based server and storage solutions. We gain advantage and differentiation through investments in higher-value capabilities, including security, scalability, and reliability, designed especially for mission-critical and AI workloads. In addition, we offer a portfolio of life-cycle services for hybrid cloud infrastructure deployment. Our principal competitors include: Dell Technologies, Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE), Intel, NetApp and Pure Storage as well as original device manufacturers (ODMs) who provide systems that are re-branded. Further, cloud service providers are leveraging innovation in technology and service delivery to compete with traditional providers and to offer additional routes to market for server and storage systems.
Financing:
Financing provides client and commercial financing, facilitating IBM clients' acquisition of hardware, software and services. Financing's ability to manage credit and residual value risk generates a competitive advantage for the company. The key competitive factors include: interest rates charged, IT product experience, client service, contract flexibility, ease of doing business, global capabilities and residual values. In client and commercial financing, Financing primarily competes with non-captive financing entities and financial institutions.
Forward-Looking and Cautionary Statements
Certain statements contained in this Form 10-K may constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 ("Reform Act"). Forward-looking statements are based on the company's current assumptions regarding future business and financial performance. These statements by their nature address matters that are uncertain to different degrees. The company may also make forward-looking statements in other reports filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), in materials delivered to stockholders and in press releases. In addition, the company's representatives may from time to time make oral forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements provide current expectations of future events based on certain assumptions and include any statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Words such as "anticipates," "believes," "expects," "estimates," "intends," "plans," "projects," and similar expressions, may identify such forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement in this Form 10-K speaks only as of the date on which it is made. Except as required by law, the company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. In accordance with the Reform Act, set forth under Item 1A. "Risk Factors" on pages 3 to 9 are cautionary statements that accompany those forward-looking statements. Readers should carefully review such cautionary statements as they identify certain important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements and from historical trends. Those cautionary statements are not exclusive and are in addition to other factors discussed elsewhere in this Form 10-K, in the company's filings with the SEC or in materials incorporated therein by reference.

IBM.quantitative_report:
**IBM Quantitative Report (2026-01-01)**

1. **Fundamental Trajectory**  
   *Revenue & Earnings*: Quarterly revenue is volatile: Q2 2025 = $16.98B, Q1 2025 = $14.54B, Q4 2024 = $17.55B, Q3 2024 = $14.97B. YoY revenue growth in Q2 2025 is +7.7% (from Q2 2024 $15.77B). TTM revenue = $64.04B. EPS diluted shows wider volatility: Q2 2025 = $2.31, Q1 2025 = $1.12, Q4 2024 = $3.11, Q3 2024 = -$0.36. YoY EPS growth in Q2 2025 is +17.9% (from Q2 2024 $1.96). TTM net income = $5.83B.  
   *Margins*: Gross margin Q2 2025 = $9.98B / $16.98B ≈ 58.8%. Operating margin Q2 2025 = $2.60B / $16.98B ≈ 15.3%. Net margin Q2 2025 = $2.19B / $16.98B ≈ 12.9%. TTM net margin = $5.83B / $64.04B ≈ 9.1%.  
   *Cash-Flow*: No cash-flow data provided.  
   *Balance Sheet*: Total assets grew from $135.24B (Q4 2023) to $148.59B (Q2 2025). Total equity grew from $22.61B to $27.59B. Total liabilities increased from $112.63B to $121.00B. Leverage (total liabilities/equity) at Q2 2025 = 121.00 / 27.59 ≈ 4.39.

2. **Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
   *Earnings Quality*: Earnings are volatile with a quarterly loss (Q3 2024 EPS = -$0.36). TTM net margin = 9.1%, ROE = 21.1%. High leverage (D/E ~4.39) amplifies ROE. No cash-flow data to assess cash conversion or accruals.  
   *Balance-Sheet Resilience*: High leverage (D/E ~4.39) relative to the equity base ($27.59B vs. $121.00B liabilities). Equity growth (from $22.61B to $27.59B over 6 quarters) is modest. No debt maturity schedule or liquidity data provided.

3. **Valuation vs. Own History**  
   Latest (2025-12-31): P/E diluted = 48.14, P/B = 10.18, P/S = 4.39, earnings yield = 2.1%.  
   2025 historical range (12 data points): P/E diluted 31.4..51.2, P/B 7.6..10.8. Current P/E is near the top of the range; P/B is at the high end. Net margin stable at ~9.1% and ROE at ~21.1% over the 2025 observation window. Valuation expansion is evident: P/E started 2025 at ~31.8 (2025-01-02) and ended at ~48.1.

4. **Price Context vs. Fundamentals**  
   *Price*: Close on 2025-12-31 = $296.21. 52-week range = $214.50–$324.90.  
   *Returns*: Annual return (since 2024-12-27) = +33.0% (from $222.78 to $296.21). Quarterly return (since 2025-10-01) = +3.4%.  
   *Fundamental Comparison*: Annual price return (+33.0%) exceeds trailing TTM EPS growth (YoY Q2 2025 EPS growth = +17.9%). Multiple expansion from ~31.8x P/E to ~48.1x P/E drives most of the price appreciation.

5. **Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**  
   *Upside*: Sustained YoY revenue growth (+7.7% in Q2 2025) and margin improvement (Q2 2025 operating margin 15.3% vs. TTM ~9.8%) could justify current valuation if continued.  
   *Downside*: High leverage (D/E ~4.39) and volatile earnings (including a quarterly loss) increase financial risk. The elevated P/E (48.1) leaves minimal margin for error; a return to the low end of its 2025 P/E range (31.4) implies ~35% downside from current levels.

6. **Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
   IBM trades at a historically high multiple (P/E 48.1 vs. 2025 range 31.4–51.2) after a +33% annual price move that outpaced trailing earnings growth. The balance sheet is highly leveraged (D/E ~4.39). Earnings quality is undermined by volatility and the absence of cash-flow data. The risk/reward is skewed to the downside given the valuation premium relative to its own history and fundamental volatility, absent evidence of durable cash generation or deleveraging.
IBM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis: IBM (Decision Date: 2026-01-01)**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2024 10-K)**
IBM is positioned as a globally integrated technology enterprise focused on enabling digital transformation through its **hybrid cloud platform and artificial intelligence (AI)**. Its business spans four segments: **Software, Consulting, Infrastructure, and Financing**. The company differentiates itself through deep industry expertise, enterprise incumbency, a broad partner ecosystem (including AWS, Microsoft, SAP), and end-to-end capabilities. IBM identifies as a leader or among the leaders in each segment, competing with hyperscalers, consultancies, and infrastructure providers based on technology innovation, performance, and integrated solutions. A forward-looking strategic focus includes emerging opportunities such as **quantum computing**.

**2. Recent Narrative Developments (Point-in-Time Evidence)**
*   **Strong Q3 2025 Performance & Raised Guidance (2025-11-12, 2025-12-20, 2025-12-27):** IBM reported Q3 2025 revenue of $16.3 billion, a 9% year-over-year increase, cited as its highest growth in several years. This was driven by high demand for AI and hybrid cloud offerings. The company raised its full-year 2025 revenue growth outlook to over 5% at constant currency.
*   **AI and Quantum Momentum (2025-12-21, 2025-12-26):** Recent narrative highlights IBM's strategic execution in quantum computing, citing its "Quantum Nighthawk" 120-qubit system and a roadmap toward fault-tolerant systems by 2029. The company is characterized as a "quantum-first" company offering a better risk-reward profile than pure-play startups. Its **AI book of business** is noted to be more than $9.5 billion.
*   **Growth Catalyst via Acquisition (2025-12-14):** The 2025 acquisition of Cognitus is framed as a potential growth catalyst, expected to deliver an estimated 3-5% incremental lift to AI consulting revenue, reinforcing IBM's growth hierarchy.
*   **Market Positioning (2025-12-16):** IBM is noted as a **dividend-paying AI stock**, providing exposure to the AI ecosystem with a yield of 2.1-2.5%, appealing to income-oriented investors.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and Regulatory Exposure (2025-11-24, 2025-12-10).** The evidence cites a proposed class-action lawsuit related to IBM's 401(k) plan fiduciaries and a mixed decision from a patent litigation appeal (IBM v. Zillow). These represent ongoing legal complexities.
    *   **Offset:** IBM's 10-K acknowledges competition and forward-looking risks but emphasizes its focus on innovation and portfolio optimization to maintain leadership. The business model is built on enterprise trust and mission-critical infrastructure.
*   **Risk: Intense and Evolving Competition (from 10-K).** IBM faces competition across all segments from hyperscalers, consultancies, and niche players. The 10-K explicitly states it is "regularly exposed to new competitors" as it executes its hybrid cloud and AI strategy.
    *   **Offset:** The narrative positions IBM's integrated platform, incumbency, and research capabilities as a structural advantage against more narrowly focused competitors.
*   **Risk: Execution on Growth Catalysts.** The positive outlook is contingent on successful integration of acquisitions (e.g., Cognitus) and achieving projected growth in AI consulting and quantum computing milestones, which are forward-looking.
    *   **Offset:** Recent quarterly performance (Q3 2025) and raised guidance provide current evidence of successful execution.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The supplied evidence presents a narrative of successful strategic execution around hybrid cloud and AI, leading to accelerating revenue growth and positive guidance. IBM's positioning in high-growth markets like quantum computing and AI, combined with its dividend, supports a stable growth thesis. However, this is balanced against ongoing legal matters and the inherent risk of executing in hyper-competitive markets. The franchise's strength lies in its integrated, enterprise-focused model.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states Q3 2025 revenue was $16.3B with 9% YoY growth, but the raw quantitative evidence only covers through Q2 2025 (filing date 2025-07-24). The Q3 2025 figure comes from news articles in the qualitative pack, not from filed financials. The quant report's latest period is Q2 2025. This creates a temporal mismatch: the qual report is citing unaudited or preliminary Q3 2025 results from press coverage, while the quant report is anchored to the latest filed 10-Q. The PM should be aware that the qual narrative is forward-looking relative to the quant evidence window.
corrections.required_action: Note to PM: The qual report's Q3 2025 revenue ($16.3B, +9% YoY) and raised guidance come from news articles, not filed financials in the quant pack. The quant analysis is anchored to Q2 2025 (latest 10-Q). This is not an error but a timing gap the PM should reconcile when combining signals.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Net margin stable at ~9.1% and ROE at ~21.1% over the 2025 observation window.' However, the raw valuation data shows net_margin ranged from 0.087 to 0.102 and ROE from 0.203 to 0.261 across the 12 data points in 2025. While not wildly volatile, calling these 'stable' overstates the case—net margin dropped from 10.2% to 8.7% before recovering to 9.1%, and ROE fell from 26.1% to 20.3%. The quant report itself notes the Q3 2024 quarterly loss (EPS = -$0.36) and earnings volatility, which is somewhat inconsistent with characterizing margins as 'stable.'
corrections.required_action: Minor: The characterization of margins as 'stable' is slightly misleading given the 10.2% to 8.7% to 9.1% trajectory. No score change needed, but PM should note the margin compression trend in early-to-mid 2025.

corrections.issue: The qual report cites IBM's 'AI book of business more than $9.5 billion' and the Cognitus acquisition delivering '3-5% incremental lift to AI consulting revenue.' These figures come from news articles in the qualitative evidence pack and are not corroborated by any filed financial data in the quant pack. The quant pack has no segment-level revenue or backlog data. While the qual report is transparent about sourcing from news, the PM should treat these forward-looking metrics with appropriate caution.
corrections.required_action: Note to PM: AI book of business ($9.5B) and Cognitus incremental lift (3-5%) are from news articles, not filed financials. Treat as directional narrative, not confirmed fundamentals.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: IBM presents a classic tension between a compelling AI/hybrid cloud/quantum narrative and deteriorating fundamental metrics at an extreme valuation. The stock's +33% annual gain has occurred alongside declining margins and ROE, while the P/E of 48.1 sits near the top of its 365-day range. The risk/reward is roughly balanced, with the narrative providing a floor but the valuation leaving minimal margin for error.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: IBM presents a classic tension between a compelling AI/hybrid cloud/quantum narrative and deteriorating fundamental metrics at an extreme valuation. The stock's +33% annual gain has occurred alongside declining margins and ROE, while the P/E of 48.1 sits near the top of its 365-day range. The risk/reward is roughly balanced, with the narrative providing a floor but the valuation leaving minimal margin for error.
key_factors: P/E of 48.14 is in the upper quartile of its 2025 range (31.4–51.2) and near the high end, while net margin has declined from 10.2% to 8.7% before recovering to 9.1% and ROE fell from 26.1% to 20.3% during the 2025 observation window, indicating valuation expansion has outpaced fundamental improvement.

Balance sheet leverage is high at 4.39x liabilities-to-equity ($121.0B liabilities vs $27.59B equity), and earnings quality is marginal given quarterly volatility including a Q3 2024 loss (EPS = -$0.36) and no cash-flow data to assess cash conversion.

AI and hybrid cloud narrative is strong with Q3 2025 revenue of $16.3B (+9% YoY, highest growth in several years) and raised full-year guidance to over 5% growth at constant currency, plus an AI book of business cited at $9.5B, though these figures come from news articles rather than filed financials.

Multiple expansion from ~31.8x P/E to ~48.1x P/E has driven most of the +33% annual price appreciation, outpacing trailing EPS growth of +17.9%, creating vulnerability to any earnings disappointment or narrative shift.

Words such as "plan," "expect," "aim," "believe," "project," "target," "anticipate," "intend," "estimate," "will," "should," "could," "would," "forecast" and other words and terms of similar meaning, typically identify such forward-looking statements. In particular, these include, among others, statements relating to:
- worldwide economic, political, regulatory, international trade, geopolitical, capital markets and other external conditions and other factors beyond the Company's control, including inflation; recession; military conflicts; trade restrictions such as sanctions, tariffs, and retaliatory measures; regulatory requirements, legal actions, or enforcement; and natural and other disasters or climate change affecting the operations of the Company or its customers and suppliers,
- foreign currency exchange rates and fluctuations in those rates,
- liabilities and the outcome of contingencies related to certain fluorochemicals known as "PFAS," including liabilities related to claims, lawsuits, and government regulatory proceedings concerning various PFAS-related products and chemistries, as well as risks related to the Company's plans to exit PFAS manufacturing and work to discontinue use of PFAS across its product portfolio,
- risks related to the class-action settlement ("PWS Settlement") to resolve claims by public water suppliers in the United States regarding PFAS,
- legal proceedings, including significant developments that could occur in the legal and regulatory proceedings described in the Company's reports on Form 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K,
- competitive conditions and customer preferences,
- the timing and market acceptance of new product and service offerings,
- the availability and cost of purchased components, compounds, raw materials and energy due to shortages, increased demand and wages, supply chain interruptions, or natural or other disasters,
- unanticipated problems or delays with the phased implementation of a global enterprise resource planning (ERP) system, or security breaches and other disruptions to the Company's information or operational technology infrastructure,
- the impact of acquisitions, strategic alliances, divestitures, and other strategic events resulting from portfolio management actions and other evolving business strategies,
- operational execution, including the extent to which the Company can realize the benefits of planned productivity improvements, as well as the impact of organizational restructuring activities,
- financial market risks that may affect the Company's funding obligations under defined benefit pension and postretirement plans,
- the Company's credit ratings and its cost of capital,
- tax-related external conditions, including changes in tax rates, laws, or regulations,
- matters relating to the spin-off of the Company's Health Care business, including the risk that the expected benefits will not be realized; the risk that the costs or dis-synergies will exceed the anticipated amounts; potential impacts on the Company's relationships with its customers, suppliers, employees, regulators and other counterparties; the ability to realize the desired tax treatment; the risk that any consents or approvals required will not be obtained; risks under the agreements and obligations entered into in connection with the spin-off, and
- matters relating to Combat Arms Earplugs ("CAE") and related products, including those related to the August 2023 settlement that is intended to resolve, to the fullest extent possible, all litigation and alleged claims involving the CAE sold or manufactured by the Company's subsidiary Aearo Technologies and certain of its affiliates ("Aearo Entities") and/or 3M ("CAE Settlement").
The Company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. Changes in such assumptions or factors could produce significantly different results.
